key_insight: Vacuum-sublimed, polycrystalline croconic acid thin films can retain robust ferroelectric behavior and deliver both piezoelectric and pyroelectric energy-harvesting functionality when protected by in situ encapsulation.
engineering_relevance: The work points to a scalable, solvent-free route for flexible, lead-free organic energy harvesters that can integrate thermal and mechanical energy conversion in one device platform.
method: Vacuum sublimation of croconic acid onto Ar plasma-treated flexible substrates
In situ encapsulation with an adamantane-based remote plasma polymer to suppress ambient degradation
Integration into multilayer piezoelectric and pyroelectric devices for macroscopic testing
evidence_signal: Piezoresponse force microscopy showed well-defined ferroelectric domains and low nanoscale coercive fields, while device tests reached power densities up to 37 microW m-2 and a pyroelectric coefficient of about 10 microC m-2 K-1.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on film thickness and device architecture, so reproducibility and long-term stability will need validation across larger-area scalable fabrication.
paper_title: Polycrystalline ferroelectric croconic acid for multisource environmental energy harvesting
